name: Eastex
description_short: Eastex Credit Union is a Texas-based financial cooperative offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards to members across Southeast Texas with branches in Evadale, Kirbyville, Silsbee, Kountze, Buna, and Spurger.
description_long:
Eastex Credit Union operates as a member-owned financial cooperative serving Southeast Texas communities. Founded to provide accessible financial services to eligible members in the region, the credit union has established a physical presence across six branch locations in East Texas. The organization is federally insured and regulated, with NMLS #806309 and routing number #313189087.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including auto loans (rates from 4.25% APR), mortgages, personal loans, share-secured loans, and specialty loans for motorcycles, ATVs, boats, RVs, and generators (up to $15K at 7.50% APR for 120 months). For banking, members access checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, IRAs, and certificate rates (up to 3.75% APY). Credit products include VISA credit cards (rates from 6.90% APR), debit/check cards, and gift cards. Additional services span mobile banking, online account access, shared branching, safe deposit boxes, cashier's checks, wire transfers, calculators, budget tools, and a TruStage insurance program.

Eastex distinguishes itself through community involvement, including a scholarship program and iSave CU youth banking initiative designed to introduce children to financial literacy before their first job. The credit union emphasizes accessibility with screen-reader compatibility, shared branching privileges, and ATM locator tools. Current promotional offerings include generator loans and rates as low as 3.50% APR on auto loans, positioning itself competitively within the regional credit union market.

As a credit union rather than a bank, Eastex operates on a not-for-profit model returning profits to members. However, potential members must meet eligibility requirements to join, limiting access compared to traditional banks. The institution serves a geographically concentrated region, which may limit convenience for those outside Southeast Texas, though shared branching mitigates this limitation somewhat. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Specialty loans (motorcycle, ATV, boat, jet ski, RV, generator)
VISA credit cards (6.90% APR starting rate)
Mobile banking and online account access
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.25% APR with flexibility for new, used, and refinanced vehicles
Specialized loan products including generator loans up to $15K at 7.50% APR for 120 months, addressing post-disaster financing needs relevant to Texas region
Youth banking program (iSave CU) allowing families to open accounts before children's first employment, promoting early financial literacy
Scholarship program actively supporting member families with educational expenses
Shared branching access allowing members to conduct transactions beyond the six physical branches
Comprehensive online and mobile banking with accessibility features including screen-reader compatibility
Member-owned credit union structure potentially offering better rates and lower fees than traditional banks
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to six branches in Southeast Texas (Evadale, Kirbyville, Silsbee, Kountze, Buna, Spurger), making in-person banking inconvenient for members outside the region
Membership eligibility requirements restrict who can join, unlike open-access traditional banks
Website does not prominently display specific fee schedules or membership eligibility criteria, requiring contact with the credit union for detailed information
Advertised rates carry disclaimers that actual rates vary based on creditworthiness, with no transparent rate calculator provided upfront
Certificate of Deposit rates (up to 3.75% APY) and IRA rates (up to 0.65% APY) are modest compared to some online-only financial institutions
best_for: Southeast Texas residents in the Hardin, Orange, and Jasper counties area seeking full-service banking with personalized local service
Families prioritizing youth financial education through the iSave CU program and scholarship opportunities
Borrowers with strong credit seeking competitive auto and mortgage loan rates without bank-level fees
Members needing emergency financing such as generator loans following severe weather events
diagnosis: Eastex Credit Union is best for Southeast Texas residents within reasonable distance of its six branches who meet membership eligibility requirements and seek competitive rates on auto loans, mortgages, and credit products combined with community-focused banking. The main caveat is that geographic service area is limited to a specific region, and membership eligibility screening may exclude some consumers; prospective members should verify eligibility and compare rates against online credit unions and regional banks before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
